title: Architect a Simple URL Shortener
tagline: Design a system that converts long URLs into short, shareable links.
scenario: A marketing team needs a branded URL shortener. They expect about 10,000 new links to be created per day, but those links might be clicked millions of times.
brief: Design the architecture for a URL shortener (like bit.ly). Focus on the database schema, the hashing algorithm used to generate the short code, and the read vs. write scaling strategy.
deliverables: A system architecture diagram (e.g., using Mermaid.js or a simple drawn diagram)
The database schema definition for the `Link` table
A brief explanation of why this system is read-heavy and how you would cache the redirects
submissionGuidance: You do not need to write the application code. Focus on the data flow: what happens when a user creates a link, and what happens when someone clicks it?

rubric.label: Hashing Logic
rubric.description: Is the short-code generation logic mathematically sound (e.g., base62)?

rubric.label: Database Schema
rubric.description: Are the database tables properly indexed for fast lookups?

rubric.label: Caching Strategy
rubric.description: Does the candidate correctly identify the need for a read cache (e.g., Redis)?
